steps.title: Set up the contrast
steps.content: The sentence says Hopper's career involved more than equations, then explains that she became a pioneering computer programmer. The word "though" marks the contrast, and a colon can introduce the explanation that follows.

steps.title: Reject faulty punctuation
steps.content:
• Choice B leaves the explanatory "as" clause attached only by commas, creating an unclear boundary.
  • Choice C starts a new sentence with "Though," but does not attach it properly to the following idea.
  • Choice D lacks the punctuation needed around the contrast and explanation.
The remaining choice uses a comma with "though" and a colon before the explanation. The answer is A.
